Overview Quick start Schema architecture Interactive samples Root schema agent-collection agents example extends extensions link presentation purpose rich-text status system-info component foundation guide pattern theme token accessibility anatomy api content design-specifications document-blocks events guideline import interaction motion principle scale section state steps variant aliases category description extends last-updated links metadata preview since status summary tags thumbnail

2 definitions in this file:

  • events
  • eventEntry
 Identifies this document block as an events spec. The events emitted by this component. Each item documents a single event with its identifier, description, return shape, DOM behavior, and lifecycle metadata.
Min items: 1 AI-ready context for this document block. Provides structured info for AI/LLM use: constraints, disambiguation, anti-patterns, examples, and keywords. References: eventEntry, agents { "kind": "events", "items": [ { "identifier": "onClick", "description": "Fires when the user activates the button. Does not fire when disabled.", "returns": "(event: MouseEvent) => void", "bubbles": true, "cancelable": true }, { "identifier": "onFocus", "description": "Fires when the button receives keyboard or programmatic focus.", "returns": "(event: FocusEvent) => void", "bubbles": true }, { "identifier": "onBlur", "description": "Fires when the button loses focus.", "returns": "(event: FocusEvent) => void", "bubbles": true } ] } The event identifier as used in code (e.g., 'onClick', 'onChange', 'onDismiss', 'ds-select'). For custom events, use the full event identifier including any namespace prefix. When this event fires, what it communicates, and how consumers should respond. Include conditions under which the event does NOT fire (e.g., 'Does not fire when disabled'). Human-readable description of the event return shape (e.g., '(event: MouseEvent) => void', '{ value: string, index: number }', 'CustomEvent<{ selectedId: string }>'). Machine-readable JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12) describing the event return or detail object. Enables tooling to generate type definitions and validate event handlers. Whether the event bubbles up through the DOM tree. Defaults to true. Set to false for events that only fire on the target element.
Default: true Whether the event can be cancelled via preventDefault(). Defaults to false. When true, document what cancelling the event prevents.
Default: false Whether the event crosses shadow DOM boundaries. Defaults to false. Relevant for web components using shadow DOM encapsulation.
Default: false The version in which this event was introduced. Whether this event is deprecated. Defaults to false.
Default: false What to use instead when this event is deprecated. Required context when deprecated is true. Usage examples showing how to listen for and handle this event. References: richText, example [ { "identifier": "onClick", "description": "Fires when the user activates the button via pointer, Enter, or Space. Does not fire when the button is disabled or in its loading state.", "returns": "(event: MouseEvent) => void", "bubbles": true, "cancelable": true }, { "identifier": "onChange", "description": "Fires when the selected value changes as a result of user input. Does not fire for programmatic value updates.", "returns": "(event: { value: string }) => void", "returnsSchema": { "type": "object", "required": [ "value" ], "properties": { "value": { "type": "string" } } }, "bubbles": true, "cancelable": false, "since": "1.2.0", "examples": [ { "title": "Listening for value changes", "presentation": { "kind": "code", "code": "<Select onChange={(e) => setValue(e.value)} />", "language": "tsx" } } ] }, { "identifier": "onDismiss", "description": "Fires when the user closes the component via the close button, the Escape key, or an outside click. Provides the reason for dismissal so consumers can respond differently per source.", "returns": "(event: { reason: 'close-button' | 'escape' | 'outside-click' }) => void", "bubbles": false, "cancelable": true, "composed": true }, { "identifier": "onToggle", "description": "Deprecated. Fires when the expanded state changes.", "returns": "(event: { expanded: boolean }) => void", "deprecated": true, "deprecationNotice": "Use onChange instead, which reports the same expanded state alongside the new value." } ]

Design System Documentation Spec (DSDS) 0.6 — Draft Specification

GitHub
